behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check
behaviors.justification: Real failure involves parsing errors or incomplete transitive resolution on actual manifest files. Unit tests with mocked parsing would miss edge cases in real YAML/JSON formats and transitive dependency resolution logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a package.json with nested dependencies, parse it with the actual scanner, verify all direct and transitive dependencies appear in the vulnerability query request

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results matching exact version ranges in the manifest, not just package name
behaviors.justification: Real failure is incorrect version range matching on actual vulnerability data. Integration test with recorded real API responses catches version matching bugs without mocking that could hide API schema issues.
behaviors.test_description: Use recorded vulnerability API responses with specific version ranges, verify the scanner correctly filters returned CVEs to those matching exact version constraints in the manifest

behaviors.description: Severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with same-severity CVEs sorted by CVSS score descending
behaviors.justification: Pure sorting logic with deterministic outcome. No I/O, API, or external dependencies. Unit test with realistic CVE data objects is sufficient to catch sorting order bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create test CVE objects with mixed severity levels and CVSS scores, call the sorting function, assert results are ordered Critical > High > Medium > Low with CVSS descending within each tier

behaviors.description: Manifest modification changes only the affected dependency version, leaving all other entries unchanged
behaviors.justification: Real failure involves file format corruption, parsing errors, or accidental modification of other entries. Mocking file I/O would miss actual parsing/writing bugs and format preservation issues.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real package.json with multiple dependencies, modify one dependency version via the scanner, verify the file remains valid and all non-target dependencies are unchanged

behaviors.description: Auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with correct single-dependency diff
behaviors.justification: Real failure involves GitHub API failures, authentication issues, rate limiting, or incorrect diff generation. Mocking the GitHub API would hide real-world failures and schema changes.
behaviors.test_description: Create an actual pull request against a real or test GitHub repository with a single-dependency version bump, verify the PR exists and diff contains only the dependency change

behaviors.description: Major version bump correctly identified as breaking change based on semver comparison
behaviors.justification: Pure semver comparison logic with deterministic output. No external dependencies or I/O. Unit test with realistic semver pairs catches comparison bugs without infrastructure overhead.
behaviors.test_description: Call the breaking-change detector with version pairs (1.0.0→2.0.0, 1.0.0→1.1.0, 1.0.0→1.0.1), verify only major version bumps are flagged as breaking

behaviors.description: When breaking change detected, PR creation is skipped and review flag is surfaced in scanner output
behaviors.justification: Real failure is PR created despite breaking change detection. Mocking PR creation could hide unmocked code paths that still create PRs. System test with real repo verifies actual behavior without mocking blind spots.
behaviors.test_description: Scan a manifest with major version bump dependency, verify no PR branch is created in the actual repository, verify scanner output contains a review flag indicating manual review required
